Empirical claim boundary

Architecture is not evidence.
Evidence is not enough.
Benchmarks decide.

Run a browser-local bridge from a GoalOS claim to a public-safe benchmark docket. Compare report-only, single-agent, unstructured swarm, fixed crew, proof-governed constellation, and Chronicle-compounding modes under equal-budget rules.

Run benchmark bridgeOpen Replay Gauntlet

No user data wanted. This page is a local public demonstration. No account. No form. No cookies. No analytics. No storage. No wallet. No network request. No token route. Do not enter personal, confidential, regulated, customer, wallet, seed phrase, private-key, or proprietary information.

BENCHMARK DOCKETWAITING
B0
single
B1
swarm
B2
fixed
B3
routed
B4
memory
B5
full
B6
external
Drealverified work / cost / risk
tasksbaselinesreplayvalidatorssafetyclaim
6task families
7baselines
0critical incidents
01

Set the benchmark claim

The bridge is local and illustrative. It teaches the evidence standard; it is not an empirical SOTA claim, not a live benchmark, and not an external audit.
02

Equal-budget baseline ladder

WAITING_FOR_RUN

Run the bridge to see whether the claim can be promoted, held, quarantined, or rejected.

Best baseline—
Candidate Δ—
Dreal—
Claim level—
03

Promotion gates

Score never bypasses gates. A candidate that wins on raw value still fails if replay, safety, baselines, public/private boundary, or human-review readiness are missing.

04

Benchmark Evidence Docket

Run the bridge to generate a BenchmarkEvidenceDocket.
01Real tasks

Benchmark claims start with task manifests, acceptance criteria, and risk class.

02Equal baselines

GoalOS must beat strong comparators under equal model, tool, budget, and safety constraints.

03Replayable proof

ProofBundles, logs, cost/risk ledgers, and validator decisions must remain inspectable.

04Bounded claim

If external replay is missing, the site says so. No unsupported empirical promotion.
